mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X5 xDrive40e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned xDrive40e (N20 PHEV) engine typically costs £5,000–£11,000 supply only. Fitting adds £900–£2,000 depending on workshop location. Main dealer new engines can exceed £10,000 supply only. Battery replacement is separate — typically £3,000–£8,000 once the warranty expires. Some owners have reported engine replacement under warranty at 74,000 miles. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X5 xDrive40e engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N20 engine itself is known for timing chain guide failure — plastic guides deteriorate and can cause catastrophic engine damage. The hybrid system adds further complexity: drivetrain malfunctions, electric coolant pump failure (Campaign 24V-608, known around 60K miles), battery degradation, rear air suspension leaks, and faulty electric air-conditioning compressors. Some owners have reported engine replacement at 74,000 miles. With proper maintenance and recall completion, these can be reliable, but they are complex vehicles.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X5 xDrive40e?

acceptedAnswer.text: N20 timing chain guide failure (rattling on cold start, potential catastrophic engine damage), drivetrain malfunction and Max eDrive disabled (often linked to electric coolant pump recall Campaign 24V-608), battery degradation (reduced EV range), rear air suspension leaks, and electric A/C compressor failure. The N20 timing chain is the most critical engine failure point.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X5 xDrive40e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% BMW scrap threshold: if your X5 is worth £10,000 and a recon engine is £7,000 (70%), it's borderline. If the vehicle is a tired £8,000 example, the same engine exceeds 70% — get a scrap valuation first. Always factor in potential battery costs (£3,000–£8,000) when making your decision.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X5 xDrive40e engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The xDrive40e uses the N20B20A (also designated N20B20O0) — a 2.0-litre turbocharged four-cylinder petrol engine with a 113 hp electric motor, producing 313 hp system output. It was fitted to the F15 X5 from 2015 to 2018. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,500–£3,500) is far cheaper than a catastrophic failure requiring a full engine replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Many specialists recommend preventative replacement at 60,000–80,000 miles regardless of symptoms on the N20. Regular oil changes every 8,000–10,000 miles are critical for N20 longevity.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X5 xDrive40e Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the only powertrain generation this badge ever used — the N20B20A PHEV. See the Era Map above for era-specific pricing. Battery replacement is separate — typically £3,000–£8,000 once the warranty expires. Some owners have reported engine replacement under warranty at 74,000 miles.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X5 xDrive40e engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The PHEV system, high-voltage battery, and integrated electric motor add significant complexity to the job.

cta.label: → Compare X5 xDrive40e engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: N20 Timing Chain Guide Failure (Petrol Engine)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or metallic noise from the engine on cold start, engine warning light, loss of power, VANOS/timing codes. Plastic timing chain guides deteriorate and can break, sending debris into the oil pan. If the chain slips or breaks, piston-valve contact can destroy the engine.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — early production N20s are most susceptible.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattling, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The N20's timing chain guides are a well-documented weak point. BMW's 15,000-mile oil change interval has been identified as a contributing factor — specialists recommend 8,000–10,000 mile intervals. If you hear any rattling from the engine, especially on cold start, book an inspection immediately. Preventative chain replacement is far cheaper than a full engine replacement.

problems.title: Drivetrain Malfunction & Max eDrive Disabled
problems.symptoms: Intermittent "Drivetrain Malfunction" warning, Max eDrive won't engage even with full battery, reduced power, automatic start/stop errors. Fault code 21A117 (Emergency Operation Manager: Request Combustion Engine) from the EME control unit.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — can affect low-mileage examples.
problems.repairCost: £500–£2,500 (diagnosis, electric coolant pump replacement, PCV system repair).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, the vehicle may become undriveable.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if warnings appear.

problems.recommendation: Campaign 24V-608 addresses leaking PCV fluid onto the electric coolant pump connector. The fix involves inspecting/replacing the pump and installing a protective shield — covered by BMW. Part #11518635089 is a known failure point around 60K miles. Related DME codes include 2E82 (cutoff) and 2E81 (speed deviation). Have the recall work checked and completed if not already done.

problems.title: Battery Degradation & Charging System Issues
problems.symptoms: Reduced EV range (real-world range often falls to 8–10 miles rather than the claimed 14–19 miles), longer charging times, battery warning lights, failure to charge.
problems.typicalMileage: 30,000–60,000 miles — accelerated by frequent rapid charging and cold weather.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — battery is a wear item; replacement is the only solution for severe degradation.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if range noticeably drops.

problems.recommendation: The 9.0–9.2 kWh battery is the hybrid system's Achilles' heel. Real-world electric range often falls short of claims. Owners report battery issues as early as 70,000 km. To maximise battery life, avoid frequent rapid charging, keep the battery between 20% and 80% for daily use, and use the BMW app to monitor battery health. High-voltage battery replacement can cost around RM 30,000.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: X5 xDrive40e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Campaign 24V-608 may cover it — check with BMW first
Only viable if the rest of the vehicle is in excellent condition
Common issue — independent workshops offer cheaper alternatives
Full engine replacement required if bottom-end or valvetrain fails

buyingChecks.title: Buying This xDrive40e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes every 8,000–10,000 miles
* Battery health report showing >85% state of health
* N20 timing chain inspected and confirmed healthy
avoidIf: * Rattling on cold start — N20 timing chain guide failure
* "Drivetrain Malfunction" or Max eDrive warnings — electric coolant pump or EME issues
* Significantly reduced EV range — battery degradation
* No service history — PHEV systems are complex and unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X5 xDrive40e is BMW's first plug-in hybrid X5 — a capable and efficient SUV but one with significant complexity. The N20 engine carries timing chain guide risks. The hybrid system has drivetrain malfunction and battery degradation vulnerabilities. Rear air suspension and electric A/C compressor failures are also common. With the 70% BMW scrap threshold, a £7,000 recon on a £10,000 xDrive40e is 70% — borderline. On a sub-£8,000 example, think carefully. Factor in potential battery costs (£3,000–£8,000) when assessing a used example.

cta.label: → Compare X5 xDrive40e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X5 xDrive40e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling on startup (N20 timing chain warning)
* Service History — Oil changes every 8,000–10,000 miles using BMW-approved oil
* Drivetrain Warnings — Any "Drivetrain Malfunction" or Max eDrive warnings?
* Campaign 24V-608 — Has the electric coolant pump recall been completed?
* EV Range — Test electric-only range; does it match expectations? (Battery degradation is common)
* Battery Health — Request a battery health report (state of health)
* Rear Air Suspension — Check for sagging rear or air spring leaks
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
